name: linSetWakeupParams
title: linSetWakeupParams
tables: This command determines the conditions under which the LIN hardware can be reactivated (leaves the Sleep mode). Default wake-up delimiter depends on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN) while the number of expected wake-up frames is 1. Main use case for this function is to simulate slow Master, which does not wake-up until an expected Wakeup frame burst. Note When LIN hardware is not in Master mode calling this function will have no effect.
Note When LIN hardware is not in Master mode calling this function will have no effect.
Note When LIN hardware is not in Master mode calling this function will have no effect.

WakeupDelimiter This parameter specifies the wake-up delimiter length, i.e. the time between the end of wake-up frame and the first sent header. Units of this parameter as well as default value depend on the hardware settings (see Hardware Configuration: LIN).Value range:: 3..255 (ms or bit times)

NumberOfWakeupFrames This parameter specifies the number of wake-up frames, after that the LIN hardware is reactivated. If the value 0 is set, the LIN hardware will never leave the Sleep mode.Value range: 0 .. 31Default value: 1
On success, a value unequal to zero, otherwise zero.

Example // simulate LIN 2.0 'slow' Master...// configure LIN hardware to wake-up after 3 wake-up frames and to send first header// 100 ms after wakeuplinSetWakeupParams(100, 3);// send wake-up frame 3 times with 150 ms delay between 2 consecutive frameslinSendWakeup(150, 3);...
